name: Have I Been Pwned API
what_it_does: API for checking whether email addresses, usernames, or passwords have appeared in known data breaches, built and maintained by Troy Hunt. Covers billions of breached credentials.
use_cases: Checking if user email addresses were exposed in known data breaches
Password hygiene enforcement by checking against breached passwords (k-anonymity model)
Alerting users when their credentials appear in new breaches
Security awareness tooling and compliance reporting
Enriching identity risk assessments in security pipelines
not_for: Real-time fraud prevention (not a fraud scoring API)
IP reputation or network-level threat intelligence
Automated bulk account scanning without subscription
best_when: An agent needs to check whether credentials or email addresses have been compromised in public data breaches, especially during account creation or login risk evaluation.
avoid_when: You need broader threat intelligence beyond breach data, or you need real-time fraud scoring.

auth.notes: API key required for breach search and paste search endpoints. Password range endpoint (k-anonymity) is free with no key. Key passed via hibp-api-key header.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Password range endpoint free; breach/paste search requires paid key
pricing.paid_tiers: Starting ~$3.50/month for personal use, higher tiers for business
pricing.notes: Pricing is tiered by number of breach searches per month. The k-anonymity password endpoint remains free for all users. Very affordable for modest use.

performance.rate_limits: 1 request per 1500ms for free, faster for paid tiers

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Returns standard HTTP status codes. 200 = breached, 404 = not found (good!), 400 for bad input, 401 for missing/invalid key, 429 for rate limiting. Simple but clear.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All endpoints are read-only GET requests — fully idempotent by nature.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate limit of 1 req/1500ms means agents must throttle breach lookups significantly
Password check uses k-anonymity — send only first 5 chars of SHA-1 hash, never the full hash
404 response means 'not found/not pwned' — agents must not treat 404 as an error
Breach data updates are not real-time — new breaches take time to be processed
Paste search is separate endpoint from breach search — requires separate calls
